message.content:
```plantuml
@startuml
title PB-749 UC4 – Update a Banner on System Management

note top of CoreNext
**Abstract:**
This use case will be responsible to update an existing banner on Next.
This endpoint is not responsible for deleting a record. To do this, it
must use the use case 5 – Delete a Banner on System Management.
However, in this use case, SM may enable or disable Banner.
end note

actor "System Management" as SM
box "Next Platform"
participant "Core Next" as CoreNext
database "Next Database" as NextDB
end box
participant "Core Bradesco" as CoreBradesco

SM -> CoreNext : 1 PUT /v1/notification/banner/{bannerID}

note left of CoreNext
System Management will call this new endpoint
passing all the fields necessary to update an
existing Banner. The fields are:
1. BANNER_ID
2. BUSINESS_EVENT_TYPE_ID
3. TEMPLATE_HEADER
4. TEMPLATE_BODY
5. ENABLE_IND
end note

CoreNext -> NextDB : 2 check if this Banner exists on BANNER table
note right of CoreNext
Core Next will check if this Banner exists on BANNER table
end note
CoreNext <-- NextDB : 3 response

alt [if the Banner exists]
    CoreNext -> NextDB : 4 check if exists an enabled Banner associated with that BUSINESS_EVENT_TYPE received on requisition on BANNER table
    note left of NextDB #FFCC66
    **Note to developers**
    Core Next will check if exists any Banner linked to a BUSINESS_EVENT_TYPE on BANNER
    table. We can only have one enabled Banner linked to a BUSINESS_EVENT_TYPE. For this
    rule, we should disregard the records that are with DELETE_FLG enabled and the Card itself.
    end note
    CoreNext <-- NextDB : 5 response

    alt [if exists an enabled Banner associated with that BUSINESS_EVENT_TYPE]
        note right of CoreNext
        If exists any Banner linked to a BUSINESS_EVENT_TYPE passed by App,
        we will throw back an error to App side.
        end note
        SM <-- CoreNext : 6 error
        note left of CoreNext #FF9999
        **HTTP code:** 422
        end note
    else [if don't exists an enabled Banner associated with that BUSINESS_EVENT_TYPE]
        note right of CoreNext
        If the defensive checks pass, then we will update the register on BANNER table.
        end note
        CoreNext -> NextDB : 7 update the register on BANNER table
        CoreNext <-- NextDB : 8 success
        SM <-- CoreNext : 9 success
        note left of CoreNext #90EE90
        **HTTP code:** 200
        end note
    end

else [if the Banner don't exists]
    SM <-- CoreNext : 10 error
    note left of CoreNext #FF9999
    If the Banner don't exists on
    BANNER table, then we will
    throw back an error to SM.
    **HTTP code:** 404
    end note
end

@enduml
```
